name: Handyman vs. General Contractor: Which Do I Need for My Project?

step.name: Step 1
step.text: It's a common question: does your project need a handyman or a full general contractor? Getting it right saves you time and money. Think of it in terms of scale and complexity. A handyman is perfect for tackling that list of smaller repairs and single-trade installations. Things like installing a new door, repairing a faulty cabinet, or replacing baseboards are right in their wheelhouse. These are typically jobs that take a few hours to a day or two and don't involve changing your home's structure.

step.name: Step 2
step.text: A general contractor (GC) comes in for the big stuff. We're talking kitchen and bathroom remodels, projects that require multiple specialized trades (plumbing, electrical, carpentry), or anything that needs a building permit. A GC manages the entire project from planning and permits to completion, ensuring everything is up to code and coordinated seamlessly. If you're removing walls or reconfiguring a space, you need a GC.

step.name: Step 3
step.text: Ultimately, the key difference often comes down to regulations. Major electrical, plumbing, and structural work legally require licensed contractors and permits. A professional service can help guide you. At City Dweller, we offer both handyman and general contractor services, so we can help you make the right call. Give us a ring to talk through your project, and we'll make sure you get the right expertise for the job.

description: Handyman vs. General Contractor: Which Do I Need for My Project?
